mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in consecutive rate-hike months are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE accelerates by >0.5pp over two consecutive months across three separate economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit growth contracts while industrial production remains elevated, it indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory buildup is financing consumption, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly lower when RSAFS declines and INDPRO stays above 103.0 for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 240, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, causing short-term equity volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as rate hike expectations dominate pricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of observations where CPIAUCSL remains above 240 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving business and consumer confidence, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as construction activity stimulates upstream demand and corporate earnings.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when HOUST increases for three consecutive months across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when monthly PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while real GDP remains stable, it indicates a shift from goods to services consumption, reducing manufacturing sector profitability and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: sectoral rotation from goods to services
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when INDPRO falls for two quarters while GDPC1 grows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, accommodative monetary conditions reduce borrowing costs for equities and leverage, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cheap capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0% for consecutive months.
